daily_brief: The escalating US-Iran conflict dominated headlines on Monday as Washington launched its ninth consecutive night of strikes against Iranian military targets, with the US death toll rising to at least three service members [BBC, Al Jazeera, The Hindu]. Oil prices surged above $91 per barrel as Iran targeted tankers in the Strait of Hormuz and extended attacks to Gulf neighbors Kuwait and Bahrain [Financial Times, France 24]. In Britain, Andy Burnham is set to become the country's seventh prime minister in a decade after King Charles III asked him to form a government, following Keir Starmer's resignation amid collapsing poll numbers [France 24, The Hindu]. Spain celebrated their second World Cup title after Ferran Torres's extra-time goal secured a 1-0 victory over Argentina in East Rutherford, with millions expected to greet the team in Madrid [BBC, RFI, Al Jazeera]. Meanwhile, Russia launched one of its most massive ballistic missile attacks on Ukrainian cities since the 2022 invasion, killing at least eight people [BBC, Le Monde]. In India, police baton-charged thousands of protesters from the Cockroach Janta Party movement attempting to march on parliament over examination irregularities [France 24, ABC Australia].

top_stories.headline: US-Iran Conflict Enters Ninth Day as American Death Toll Rises and Gulf States Come Under Fire
bullets: US Central Command confirmed a service member was killed in northern Iraq during a controlled detonation of unexploded ordnance from a downed Iranian drone, with a second service member wounded
The US launched its ninth consecutive night of strikes targeting Iranian military command centers, air defense sites, maritime capabilities, and communications networks
Iran's Revolutionary Guards reported two oil tankers 'exploded' in the Strait of Hormuz; Kuwait and Bahrain said their air defenses responded to Iranian attacks
Oil prices surged above $91 per barrel—the highest since June 11—as the US death toll in the conflict reached at least 17

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized US military casualties and operational details; reported US death toll at 17

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Highlighted diplomatic channels remaining open via mediators; covered Iran's call for IAEA condemnation of strikes on nuclear site

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Focused on Iranian perspective and IRGC statements; detailed attacks on Jordan

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Emphasized oil price impacts and Secretary Rubio's statements on Strait of Hormuz leverage

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Led with oil prices and economic implications

top_stories.headline: Andy Burnham to Become UK's Seventh Prime Minister in a Decade After Starmer's Fall
bullets: King Charles III asked former Greater Manchester mayor Andy Burnham to form a government following Keir Starmer's resignation after just two years in office
Burnham won a by-election in Makerfield on June 18 and was sworn in as MP on June 22, enabling his path to leadership
Burnham will be the UK's first lifelong Catholic prime minister; he is expected to announce senior cabinet appointments Monday
Starmer left office with the worst poll ratings of his tenure, having lost confidence of both the public and his own party

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Focused on Burnham's incoming priorities: restoring confidence, delivering growth, and devolving power

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Provided extensive biographical context; noted Burnham is the sixth PM to resign post-Brexit

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Headline focused on cabinet appointment challenges ahead

top_stories.headline: Spain Win Second World Cup Title with Extra-Time Victory Over Argentina
bullets: Ferran Torres scored 37 seconds into the second period of extra time to give Spain a 1-0 victory over Argentina in East Rutherford, New Jersey
Argentina's Enzo Fernandez was sent off in the 93rd minute for a second yellow card; Spain dominated with 65% possession and 20 shots on goal
Spain conceded only one goal in the entire tournament—the fewest by any World Cup-winning team
A million fans are expected to greet the team in Madrid; celebrations were marred by the death of a teenager when a fountain collapsed in western Spain

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Spain's footballing dominance and called it a 'victory for football'

sources.name: RFI
sources.framing_note: Covered homecoming celebrations and reported the death during festivities

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Noted Trump was booed during trophy presentation; detailed tactical analysis

top_stories.headline: Russia Launches One of Most Massive Ballistic Missile Attacks on Ukraine Since 2022 Invasion
bullets: At least eight people were killed across Kharkiv, Kyiv, Zaporizhzhia, and Sumy in coordinated ballistic missile and drone strikes
President Zelensky described the raid on Kyiv as 'one of the most massive ballistic attacks' since the full-scale invasion began
Ukrainian military said air defenses shot down 18 of 41 missiles and intercepted 108 drones; five more died when a Turkish-owned grain ship was hit in the Black Sea
Ukraine struck back, hitting three Russian oil tankers in the Black Sea and three oil depots in Russia's Stavropol region

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Detailed casualty figures and quoted Zelensky on scale of attack

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Article accessible but content could not be fully loaded

top_stories.headline: Indian Police Baton-Charge 'Cockroach Party' Protesters Attempting March on Parliament
bullets: Thousands of protesters gathered in New Delhi despite a ban on the march, demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over examination irregularities
Police in riot gear charged protesters near the Jantar Mantar site; the movement began after national medical exam papers were leaked in May
Activist Sonam Wangchuk was forcibly moved from a hunger strike to hospital on Saturday, galvanizing thousands more supporters
The Cockroach Janta Party says government representatives reached out for talks Monday morning; two members were reportedly en route to meet a senior minister

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized police action and protest demands; noted it is biggest challenge to Modi in his third term

sources.name: ABC Australia
sources.framing_note: Described police using canes; highlighted social media mobilization of millions

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Article headline visible but content could not be fully loaded

additional_stories.headline: Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in US after UK authorities issue 38 new charges including rape and sex trafficking, bringing total to 59
Norway unveils new memorial for 77 victims of 2011 Breivik terror attacks ahead of 15th anniversary
Wildfire north of Madrid scorches 12,000 hectares as Spain battles multiple blazes amid climate-driven heatwaves
France's controversial 'Ripost' public disorder law faces final parliamentary committee vote with left opposition united against it
Council of Europe urges FIFA to strengthen integrity framework ahead of 2030 World Cup, citing political pressure and betting concerns
China's state-backed funds buy $9 billion in shares to prop up struggling stock market
Russians increasingly turn to cash as drone-related internet shutdowns disrupt card payments and businesses dodge taxes
Vice President J.D. Vance and wife Usha welcome fourth child, first baby born to sitting US VP in over 150 years
Singapore minister Faishal Ibrahim resigns over 'interactions with a woman' as ruling party faces another scandal
Food delivery rider Wang Jibing wins China's prestigious Lu Xun Literary Prize for poetry collection written between deliveries
